Sourdough Bread Guide: A healthy sourdough starter is the foundation of good sourdough bread — feed it equal weights flour and water every 12–24 hours until it reliably doubles in size within 4–8 hours. For the dough, combine 450g bread flour, 325g water, 90g active starter, and 9g salt; perform a series of stretch-and-fold cycles over a 4-hour bulk fermentation at room temperature. Shape the dough, place seam-side up in a floured banneton, and cold-proof in the refrigerator overnight for 12–16 hours to develop flavor. Bake in a preheated Dutch oven at 500°F (260°C) for 20 minutes covered, then 25 minutes uncovered for a deep crust.
